questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi was the last sultan of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was the last sultan of the Lodi dynasty.

questions.q: Who defeated and killed Ibrahim Lodi in battle?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was defeated and killed by Babur at the First Battle of Panipat.

questions.q: At which 1526 battle was Ibrahim Lodi defeated and slain?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi fell at the First Battle of Panipat in 1526 CE.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi was the last sultan of which medieval Indian state?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was the last sultan of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi's defeat opened the way for which empire in northern India?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi's death cleared the way for Mughal rule in northern India.

questions.q: In which year did Ibrahim Lodi die at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was killed at the First Battle of Panipat in 1526 CE.

questions.q: From which city did Ibrahim Lodi rule as sultan?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi was the sultan of Delhi, capital of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: The Lodi dynasty that Ibrahim led was of which broad ethnic origin?
questions.explanation: The Lodi dynasty was of Afghan origin, the last to rule the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi is chiefly remembered as the sultan whose fall marked the end of what era?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi's defeat ended the long era of the Delhi Sultanate.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi belonged to which century of Indian history?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi ruled and died in the early 16th century CE.

questions.q: Whom did Ibrahim Lodi succeed as sultan of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi succeeded his father Sikandar Lodi as sultan in 1517.

questions.q: Who was the founder of the Lodi dynasty, the grandfather of Ibrahim?
questions.explanation: Bahlul Lodi founded the Lodi dynasty; Ibrahim was his grandson via Sikandar Lodi.

questions.q: Which discontented governor of the Punjab is traditionally said to have invited Babur to invade?
questions.explanation: Daulat Khan Lodi, governor of the Punjab, is said to have invited Babur against Ibrahim.

questions.q: What chiefly weakened Ibrahim Lodi's position before Panipat?
options: Revolts by his own Afghan nobles
questions.explanation: Ibrahim alienated and faced revolts from his Afghan nobles, undermining his rule before Panipat.

questions.q: Despite a much larger army, why did Ibrahim Lodi lose at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur's field artillery and flanking tactics overcame Ibrahim's numerically larger force.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi holds the grim distinction of being which kind of Delhi sultan?
options: The only Delhi sultan known to have died on the battlefield
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi is noted as the only Sultan of Delhi to die fighting on the battlefield.

questions.q: Which Rajput ruler also opposed Ibrahim Lodi and clashed with the Lodis before Babur's arrival?
questions.explanation: Rana Sanga of Mewar had fought the Lodis and was a rising power in Ibrahim's time.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi's capital and the seat of Lodi power had been shifted toward which city by Sikandar Lodi?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi founded and developed Agra, which became central to Lodi rule.

questions.q: Roughly how long did Ibrahim Lodi reign before his death at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi reigned from 1517 to 1526, roughly nine years.

questions.q: What did Ibrahim Lodi's attempts to centralise authority provoke among the nobility?
questions.explanation: His efforts to assert strong central control bred rebellion and resentment among Afghan nobles.

questions.q: The Lodi Gardens in Delhi today contain tombs associated with which dynasty's rulers?
questions.explanation: The Lodi Gardens in Delhi preserve tombs of Lodi and Sayyid rulers.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi's defeat is generally taken to mark the transition from sultanate rule to which new order in Delhi?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim's fall marked the shift from the Delhi Sultanate to Mughal rule under Babur.

questions.q: In which year did Ibrahim Lodi ascend the throne of Delhi?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi became sultan in 1517 CE on the death of his father Sikandar Lodi.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi briefly tried to share power with which relative before turning against him?
questions.explanation: Ibrahim's nobles briefly raised his brother Jalal Khan at Jaunpur before Ibrahim removed him.

questions.q: Which rebellious Lodi noble in the east, governor of Bihar, defied Ibrahim and later figured in the rise of Sher Shah?
options: Dariya Khan and the Afghan chiefs of the east
questions.explanation: The eastern Afghan chiefs, among whom Sher Khan rose, defied Ibrahim's authority in Bihar.

questions.q: According to Babur's own account, roughly how many men did Ibrahim Lodi field at Panipat?
questions.explanation: Babur's Baburnama puts Ibrahim's army at roughly 100,000 men with many war elephants.

questions.q: Where is Ibrahim Lodi traditionally said to be buried?
options: In the Lodi Gardens in Delhi
questions.explanation: Ibrahim Lodi's tomb is traditionally identified at Panipat, near where he fell.

questions.q: Ibrahim's father Sikandar Lodi is also remembered for founding which city, later central to the Mughals?
questions.explanation: Sikandar Lodi founded Agra in 1504, which the Mughals later made a chief capital.

questions.q: Which battlefield innovation by Babur, a defensive line of carts, helped break Ibrahim's charge?
questions.explanation: Babur lashed carts together in an araba line to anchor his guns and blunt Ibrahim's advance.

questions.q: Ibrahim Lodi's defeat at Panipat is often cited as an early Indian example of the decisive use of what?
questions.explanation: Panipat is a landmark example of gunpowder field artillery deciding a major Indian battle.
